content.full_text:
June 6, 1990

Teresa Craigie Lahaderne  
Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro  
P.O. Box 7880  
San Francisco, Ca. 94120  

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance  
Our File No. I-90-259

Dear Ms. Lahaderne:

You have requested informal assistance under the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ Because the question you have asked is of a general nature, we treat your request as one for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed), rather than formal advice.²

QUESTION

Does a corporate employee who delivers a campaign contribution to a candidate or recipient committee on behalf of a political committee sponsored by his or her corporate employer qualify as an "intermediary" under the Act?

CONCLUSION

A corporate employee who delivers a campaign contribution to a candidate or recipient committee on behalf of a political committee sponsored by his or her corporate employer does not qualify as an "intermediary" under the Act.

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by a opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

ANALYSIS

Section 84302.5 specifically excludes from the definition of "intermediary" for transmittal of a contribution a person who delivers to a candidate or committee a contribution from his or her employer, or an association to which the person belongs. We believe this exception applies to a person who delivers a contribution from his or her employer's sponsored committee or a committee sponsored by an association to which he or she belongs. At its November 7, 1989 meeting, the Commission approved for adoption Regulation 18432.5 (copy enclosed) which interprets and clarifies Section 84302.5.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Sandra L. Taylor
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure
